overview.description: AI-powered diagnostics for neurodegenerative diseases (Alzheimer's, Parkinson's)
overview.description_long: Neurona Lab is a Prague-based health-tech company building AI algorithms for fast and precise diagnosis of neurodegenerative diseases. Their product suite includes Neurona PET (MRI & PET fusion algorithm for amyloid quantification), Neurona ARIA (detection of cerebral oedemas and microbleeding — complications of Alzheimer's biological treatments), and Neurona App (speech-based early Alzheimer's/Parkinson's detection). All products are progressing through clinical testing and CE medical-device certification. Part of the Channel Lab group.
overview.tagline: Fast and precise diagnosis of neurodegenerative diseases using artificial intelligence.

history.origin_story: Founded by Jan Pavlík (also co-founder of Terrapino) to apply AI to the growing global dementia crisis; WHO estimates 55M+ sufferers worldwide, with primary care identifying fewer than 1 in 3 patients.

key_milestones.event: Presented products at CzechCrunch Longevity Fest in Prague
Attended AD/PD 2026 international Alzheimer's & Parkinson's conference in Copenhagen; secured partnerships for Neurona PET

notable_people.name: Prof. MUDr. Jakub Hort, Ph.D, FEAN

partnerships: Motol University Hospital (clinical testing partner — inferred from advisory board)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: Neurona Lab builds CE-certified medical-device AI for neurodegenerative diagnostics. Regulatory certification, clinical trial data, and integration with hospital information systems (DICOM/HL7) create a deep compliance moat that foundation models cannot replicate off-the-shelf.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: The neurodegenerative diagnostics market is large and growing (55M→139M dementia patients by 2050), but Neurona Lab is pre-commercial and competing with established medical imaging vendors (Siemens, GE, Philips). Revenue timeline depends on CE certification cycles.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No public funding information found. The company is 2–10 employees, pre-commercial (products still in R&D/certification phases), and relies on the Channel Lab group umbrella. Without confirmed external investment, runway is uncertain.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Strong regulatory moat and a large unmet clinical need offset the pre-commercial status and funding uncertainty. Success depends on navigating CE/MDR certification timelines and securing hospital partnerships — achievable given the experienced medical advisory board.

metadata.notes: Input category is 'research_group' but Neurona Lab describes itself as a 'health-tech company'; classified here as startup/product given commercial intent and product roadmap. Address: Vodičkova 17, Prague 1. LinkedIn shows 2,315 followers and 2-10 employees. Member of Channel Lab group (parent/incubator).
